methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $15.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.1B - $250.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Dataminr
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series F), post-money $4.1B

data.narrative:
Applied Intuition, a Series F AI & Software company, carries a current post-money valuation of $15.0B following $600M+ in total capital raised. The valuation assessment draws on a comparables universe of 8 private-stage peers in the AI & Software sector, supplemented by 1 public reference (Palantir Technologies, ticker PLTR). Key private comparables include Dataminr (Series F, $4.1B), SandboxAQ (Series E, $5.6B), Crusoe Energy (Series E, $10.0B), and xAI (Series E, $250.0B).

Two triangulation methods yield meaningfully different results. The stage-progression method, applying a typical 1.3x Series F step-up multiplier to the $15.0B base, implies a valuation of $19.5B. The peer-median approach, anchored to the median valuation across 8 same-sector comparables, implies $3.9B—though the peer set exhibits substantial dispersion (range: $1.1B to $250.0B). This wide range reflects acute valuation heterogeneity within frontier AI.

Synthesizing these methods yields a triangulated range of $3.3B to $22.4B, with a median estimate of $11.7B. The current $15.0B valuation sits in the upper-middle portion of this range. Key uncertainties include the appropriate stage-step multiplier in an inflated AI market, the relevance of the peer median given the outlier effect of xAI's $250B valuation, and the distinction between Applied Intuition's autonomous vehicle software focus and the broader AI comparables set.
